data.title: Wheat futures rise as USDA cuts outlook after weak winter crop
data.description:
U.S. wheat and corn futures rose sharply for the second straight session Wednesday after the U.S. Department of Agriculture released its annual Acreage report and its quarterly Grain Stocks report.

Wheat led the CBOT on projections of a tighter global supply-and-demand picture due to a shortfall in U.S. winter wheat, as well as the effects of heat in France and potential problems with the Russian crop.

However, "traders still need a stronger bullish fundamental story for a sustained rebound, especially with weather forecasts supporting strong yield potential," Joe Davis of Futures International said in a note.

On Wednesday, CBOT wheat (W_1:COM) for September delivery jumped 2% to $6.01 per bushel, corn (C_1:COM) for December delivery added 1.6% to $4.43 per bushel, and soybeans (S_1:COM) for November delivery edged uo 0.5% to $11.49 1/2 per bushel.

The USDA's annual Acreage report showed total wheat acreage declining 6% Y/Y to a less-than-expected 42.7M acres, and the government's Grain Stocks report revealed a lower-than-forecast 920M bushels of old crop wheat in U.S. inventory as of June 1.

"Small acres on a struggling crop is the friendliest setup in the report, in our view," Jake Hanley of Teucrium Trading said in a note, adding the data suggests that supply and demand for U.S. wheat is tighter than soybeans and corn, which seem more relaxed.

The latest USDA Crop Progress report published Monday said just 26% of the U.S. winter crop was in good or excellent condition, down from 48% at the same time a year ago.

ETFs: (WEAT), (CORN), (SOYB), (DBA), (MOO)

data.title: Cleveland-Cliffs wins $400M U.S. Defense electrical steel contract
data.description:
• Cleveland-Cliffs Steel, a unit of Cleveland-Cliffs (CLF), won a maximum $400M contract for grain-oriented electrical steel.
  • The contract is a five-year agreement with no option periods, with the performance completion date set for Sept. 8, 2030.
  • The contract supports multiple U.S. military services, including the Army, Marine Corps, Navy, Air Force, and Space Force.
  • The contract is funded under fiscal 2025–2029 transaction funds and awarded through the Defense Logistics Agency Contracting Services Office, Ohio.

data.title: Vertex gains expanded indication for Casgevy in children as young as two
data.description:
• The US FDA has approved Vertex Pharmaceuticals' (VRTX) gene therapy Casgevy (exagamglogene autotemcel) for children as young as two years old with sickle cell disease or β thalassemia.
  • The approval is the eighth under the Commissioner's National Priority Voucher pilot program.
  • The treatment had previously been approved for individuals 12 and older.

data.title: Lockheed Martin's segment wins $347.5M deal for prototype defense system work
data.description:
• Lockheed Martin (LMT) announced on Wednesday that its Missile and Fire Control segment won a $347.5M cost-plus-incentive-fee contract from the U.S. Army.
  • The contract covers the development, fabrication, and testing of improvements to prototype air and missile defense systems.
  • The work locations and funding will be determined with each order, while the project is expected to be completed by Dec. 31, 2028.
  • Army Contracting Command is serving as the contracting activity.

data.title: AeroVironment wins $500M U.S. Army contract to supply counter-drone systems
data.description:
• AeroVironment (AVAV) announced on Wednesday that it secured a $500M firm-fixed-price contract to provide commercial counter-drone and counter-small-drone systems for the procurement of counter-UAS capabilities.
  • The work locations and funding will be assigned separately under individual orders.
  • The project is expected to run through June 29, 2029, with Army Contracting Command serving as the contracting activity.

data.title: Centrus chalks out details of $900M HALEU enrichment contract with Department of Energy
data.description:
Centrus Energy (LEU) has finalized the terms of a competitively awarded $900 million task order it received from the U.S. Department of Energy earlier this year for the deployment of large-scale production capacity for High-Assay, Low-Enriched Uranium (HALEU).

Centrus and the department have signed a three-month, $15M extension for HALEU storage. The company has now completed all HALEU production called for under the existing demonstration contract. Production of the final 900 kilograms of HALEU UF6 required under that contract was completed in mid-June, two weeks ahead of schedule, with a cumulative total of more than 1,900 kilograms produced over the life of the contract. 

The new, fixed-price HALEU enrichment contract calls for Centrus to deploy commercial-scale HALEU production capacity in Piketon. It also includes options, at the department's discretion, for up to $170 million in HALEU purchases for departmental missions; the total contract value with all options included is $1.07 billion. 

The initial build-out will include 12 metric tons of annual HALEU production capacity as well as capacity to meet Centrus' existing LEU backlog of $2.4 billion. Subject to customer demand, Centrus can continue expanding production of HALEU and LEU to meet market requirements. Importantly, Centrus expects the initial build-out to allow it to achieve nth-of-a-kind centrifuge manufacturing costs.

Centrus had originally won the contract in 2019 to build a cascade of advanced centrifuges in Piketon to demonstrate HALEU production with U.S. technology. That demonstration contract was modified and extended in 2022 to allow for a longer period of HALEU production and was previously extended through June 30, 2026.

LEU +1.98% to $169.64.

Source: Press Release

data.title: Meridian3 Industrials prices $175M IPO
data.description:
• Meridian3 Industrials Acquisition (MIACU) priced its initial public offering of 17.5M units at $10/unit, raising $175M.
  • The units are expected to begin trading on Nasdaq on July 2, 2026.
  • Each unit consists of one Class A ordinary share and one-half of one redeemable warrant, with each whole warrant exercisable at $11.50/share.
  • The IPO is expected to close on July 6, 2026.
  • The underwriter has a 45-day option to purchase up to an additional 2.625M units to cover over-allotments.

data.title: Oscar Health stock turns "$35.7M insider selling" into a new 52-week high
data.description:
Oscar Health (OSCR) stock rallied on Wednesday after investors digested a newly released SEC Form 4 filing showing CEO and Director Mark T. Bertolini sold 1.24M Class A shares worth about $35.7M.

The Oscar Health stock jumped as much as ~12.23% during the session to a new 52-week high of around $32.06 before closing at ~$31.90, as investors looked past the headline figure and focused on the reason behind the transactions.

The filing showed the transactions were entirely non-discretionary and linked to tax obligations rather than a change in management's view on the company.

According to the Form 4 footnotes, the shares were automatically sold to cover tax withholding obligations related to the deferred settlement of PSUs that vested on April 3, 2026.

The transactions were made under a pre-arranged Rule 10b5-1 trading plan adopted in November 2025 and updated in March 2026, indicating the sales were not driven by market timing or concerns about the business.

Investors also viewed the filing as confirmation that the entire $35.7M selling program had already been completed and absorbed by the market, removing a potential overhang on the stock.

On the market side, Oscar Health's latest breakout extended its strong 2026 performance, with the stock up 121.99% year-to-date, far ahead of the 9.60% return delivered by the S&P 500 (SP500).

The stock currently carries a Strong Buy Quant Rating of 4.92, while Seeking Alpha analysts rate it Buy at 3.75, and Wall Street analysts maintain a Hold rating of 3.18.

data.title: Securitize stock to start trading July 2 after deal with Cantor Equity Partners II closed
data.description:
Securitize completed its previously announced business combination with Cantor Equity Partners II  (CEPT), allowing the tokenization platform's common stock to start trading on the New York Stock Exchange on Thursday, July 2, 2026, under the ticker "SECZ," the companies said Wednesday.

Cantor Equity Partners II (CEPT) stock rose 2.0% in after-hours trading.

Securitize has brought more than $4B in assets onchain. Its public listing will help the firm's platform to further expand.

"Becoming a public company enables us to continue building the infrastructure institutions and investors need as more capital markets move onchain," said Securitize CEO Carlos Domingo.

Since announcing the proposed business combination, Securitize has expanded its institutional platform through collaborations with the New York Stock Exchange (ICE), Computershare, Continental, and other major market participants, while deepening relationships with the world's largest asset managers, including BlackRock (BLK).
